member.name: Anneliese Dodds

addresses: Unit A, Bishop Mews, Transport Way, Oxford, OX4 6HD

member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Anneliese Dodds is the Labour (Co-op) MP for Oxford East , and has been an MP continually since 8 June 2017.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Sovereign AI Fund
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, if she will provide details of conditions attached to awards, including a) commitments regarding UK headquarters and office presence, b) commitments regarding UK-based hiring and job cr
writtenQuestions.answerText: Sovereign AI is designed to help the most promising UK AI companies start, scale and remain anchored in the UK. Before any equity investment is made, the Fund assesses a company’s current and future UK nexus, including domicile, headquarters and key execu...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Infant Foods: Labelling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether her department has objected to the May 2026 Codex circular letter covering labelling standards for emergencies, on the grounds that it should not be applied to baby products.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK did not object to the food labelling provisions in emergencies guidelines via circular letter. Defra engaged actively throughout the Codex process, working with Members to strengthen consumer protection. The UK secured clarification that any tempor...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Sudan: Conflict Resolution
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of a dedicated Women, Peace and Security mediation track, ensuring the inclusion of Sudanese women in civilian engagement
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK is committed to ensuring women's full, equal, meaningful and safe participation in any future peace process in Sudan, and the Foreign Secretary has consistently worked to elevate the voices of Sudanese women in international fora. The UK offers its...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sudan: Peace Negotiations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps she is taking to support and promote the African Union’s role in ceasefire and confidence building measure negotiations for Sudan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The African Union has an important role to play in the search for a solution to the Sudan conflict, including as a member of the Quintet of multilateral organisations (UN, EU, AU, IGAD, LAS). We support the Quintet's efforts to establish a civilian-led, i...

writtenQuestions.heading: Muammar Ibrahim
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what representations she has made to the RSF on the detention of Muammar Ibrahim.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK strongly condemns reports of deliberate attacks on human rights defenders and journalists across Sudan. In October 2023, the UK led the charge to establish the UN Fact-Finding Mission (FFM), which has been renewed for two consecutive years with UK ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners' Release
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what information his department holds about regional differences in the prevalence of Friday evening and weekend prison discharge rates.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Friday Releases scheme was brought into force on 30 November 2023, introducing a presumption against releasing offenders on a Friday, therefore reducing risks linked to limited weekend services and helping keep the public safer by lowering the reoffen...

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners' Release: Reoffenders
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what is his assessment of the potential impact of Friday evening and weekend prison discharges on reoffending.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Friday Releases scheme was brought into force on 30 November 2023, introducing a presumption against releasing offenders on a Friday, therefore reducing risks linked to limited weekend services and helping keep the public safer by lowering the reoffen...

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners' Release
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what estimate he has made of the number of prison discharges taking place on a Friday evening or weekend.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Friday Releases scheme was brought into force on 30 November 2023, introducing a presumption against releasing offenders on a Friday, therefore reducing risks linked to limited weekend services and helping keep the public safer by lowering the reoffen...

writtenQuestions.heading: X Corp: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what discussions she has had with X on removing illegal material in different instances relating to how it becomes aware of that material.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Secretary of State has repeatedly called out illegal content on X including when the Grok AI tool was being used to create and share degrading, non-consensual intimate deepfakes.Government engages regularly with online platforms, including X, to press...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Bicycles: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what is her assessment of the percentage of e-bikes on British roads which are not compliant with the EAPC regulations concerning a) electric motors exceeding 250 watts and b) possessing electrical assistance w
writtenQuestions.answerText: No such assessment has been made. The Department has published advice to the public on the EAPC Regulations and the police have the powers to seize such vehicles where they are used on the roads without properly being registered as road vehicles.

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Bicycles: Registration
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what is her estimate of the number of e-bikes registered as road vehicles in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No such estimate has been made. Advice for the public on the rules concerning e-bikes and when it is necessary to register them as road vehicles – in order to be legally used on the roads – has been published by the Department.

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Bicycles: Speed Limits
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps is her department taking to help ensure that e-bikes are not exceeding 15.5mph.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has published information setting out the rules with regards to e-bikes. Where e-bikes continue to receive electrical assistance above 15.5mph they are classed as motor vehicles and must be approved and registered with the Driver and Vehicl...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Bicycles: Batteries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what actions have her department taken regarding the online sale of lithium-ion batteries that could be used in an unsafe manner on e-bikes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: My Department has previously published guidance to consumers on e-bike battery safety. More recently, the Department for Business and Trade has consulted on regulations to address the sale of unsafe products bought online. This follows the introduction of...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Bicycles: Delivery Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, with reference to the Answer of 5 March 2026 to Question 117052 on Electric Bicycles: Delivery Services, whether the National Work-Related Road Safety Charter has been produced and, if not, what ongoing work is
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has committed to developing and piloting a new Work-Related Road Safety Charter for all those who drive or ride for work. The charter is in the early stages of development with the input of a wide range of stakeholders including road safety...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Bicycles: Delivery Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, with reference to the Answer of 9 July 2025 to Question 65474 on Electric Bicycles: Delivery Services, what response her department received about the actions taken to comply with relevant legal requirements an
writtenQuestions.answerText: Responses were received from all three delivery platforms that were written to. They said they require riders to comply with legal requirements and the Highway Code, provide safety guidance and training on safe vehicle use, expect e-bikes and other vehicl...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Bicycles: Delivery Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, with reference to the Answer of 9 July 2025 to Question 65474 on Electric Bicycles: Delivery Services, which food delivery companies were contacted.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The then Minister for Local Transport wrote to Deliveroo, Just Eat and Uber Eats about the legality of the e-bikes used by food delivery riders. A copy of those letters is available in the Library of the House.

writtenQuestions.heading: Sudan: Armed Conflict
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment she has made of the implications for her policies of the situation in El Obeid, Sudan; and what steps her Department is taking to help prevent atrocities ther
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK Government is working intensively and urgently with international partners to try and prevent the loss of civilian life in El Obeid. On 18 June, the UK joined our partners in the Coalition for Atrocity Prevention and Justice at the UN Human Rights ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, pursuant to her written statement of 2 March 2026 on Asylum changes, by what date her Department intends to have operationalised a Named Community Sponsorship scheme for refugee resettlement.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In the Restoring Order and Control statement, published in November 2025, the Government committed to transforming its approach to safe and legal routes. This included the creation of a named sponsorship scheme and capped routes for refugee and displaced ...

Name of donor: Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung London Limited Address of donor: 23 Devereux Court, London WC2R 3JJ Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (£325.37) and accommodation (£182.45), value £507.82 Destination of visit: Germany Dates of visit: 14 October 2025 to 16 October 2025 Purpose of visit: Participation in annual UK-German Parliamentary Dialogue organised by the Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung. (Registered 16 October 2025)

Name of donor: Energy & Climate Intelligence Unit Address of donor: 180 Borough High Street, London SE1 1LB Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Accommodation (£539.91), flights (£2,271.72) and maximum subsistence received (£383.64), value £3,195.17 Destination of visit: Brazil (Belém, Pará) Dates of visit: 11 November 2025 to 17 November 2025 Purpose of visit: Attendance of COP30 to meet legislators and experts and to build knowledge of the international climate process. (Registered 1 December 2025)

Name of donor: Action Against Hunger Address of donor: Floor 11, Exchange Tower, 1 Harbour Exchange Square, London E14 9GE Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (£3441), accommodation (£618), meals and subsistence (£199), transport (£193), security (£53), and other (£121), value £4,625 Destination of visit: Chad Dates of visit: 24 May 2026 to 30 May 2026 Purpose of visit: Visit to Chad organised and funded by Action Against Hunger (registered charity no. 1047501), in partnership with the APPG for Sudan and South Sudan. The visit aimed to provide an insight into humanitarian conditions in Chad, including the impact of displacement from Sudan, and to observe Action Against Hunger’s work, alongside that of other partners including UK-funded programmes, and to meet with a range of stakeholders and affected communities. (Registered 23 June 2026)
